Our Robot
About the program
What is FIRST Robotics Competition?

FRC is sometimes called the "varsity sport for the mind." Teams design, build, and program a robot from scratch, then take it into a live competition arena.

01
Design & Build
Engineer a real robot from scratch using advanced tools over the course of the program.
02
Program & Test
Write Java code to control your robot autonomously. See your code come to life in real time.
03
Compete
Head to K-Days in Edmonton and go up against other teams in a high-energy arena.
04
Join the Team
This program is your on-ramp to joining Team 9449 YellowJackets for the full season next year.

No experience necessary.

You don't need to know anything about robots or coding to join. That's kind of the whole point. We'll teach you everything from scratch.

Skills you'll gain
What You'll Learn

Over the course of the program you'll pick up a handful of skills that go well beyond robotics.

CAD Design
Use Computer Aided Design software to model and plan robot components before you build anything physical.
Java Programming
Write real Java code to control your robot. It's one of the most widely used programming languages out there.
Electrical Wiring
Wire motors, sensors, and control systems together safely so your robot actually does what you want it to.
Team Building
Work alongside other students to solve real problems under real time pressure. It's harder than it sounds, and a lot more fun.
Electrical wiring in the build space
CAD design on Onshape
Important dates
Program Schedule

Here is how the summer breaks down, from the first weekend session to the competition floor at K-Days.

June 6, 2025
Program kicks off
First weekend session. Meet your team, get your bearings, and start building.
June 6 to July 19 · Weekends
Weekend workshops
You'll spend weekends designing, programming, wiring, and testing your robot with the rest of the group and some solid mentors.
July 17 to 19, 2025
K-Days Competition in Edmonton
Compete at the FIRST Robotics Competition hosted by First Alberta at K-Days. Everything you built over the summer comes together here.
After the program
Join Team 9449 next season
Finishing the Try FRC program puts you in a great spot to try out for the full 9449 YellowJackets roster the following year.
The team
Who You'll Be Joining

The YellowJackets are a group of students who take robotics seriously and have a lot of fun doing it. This program is how most of them got started.

Ready to build?

Just $75 per person. Weekends starting June 6. No experience needed, just bring some curiosity.
